label: Datasets, sampling & splits

node.label: Datasets, sampling & splits
node.definition: Examples, populations, sampling schemes, train/validation/test splits, and provenance determine what a model can learn and how it is measured.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Datasets, sampling & splits is modeled as depending on Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design and feeding downstream nodes such as Multimodal, simulated & streaming data, Supervised learning, Unsupervised learning.
examples: atlas downstream link to Multimodal, simulated & streaming data
atlas downstream link to Supervised learning

limitations: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design.

dependencies.rationale: Datasets, sampling & splits rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.

In this atlas graph, Datasets, sampling & splits has a `requires` dependency on Databases & information retrieval with `common` strength.
